title: Applied History — lab for using historical case study to inform contemporary policy

fields.vision: An applied-history lab for using historical cases as disciplined analytic instruments.
fields.mechanism: It connects portfolio-native case selection, Belfer-style applied-history logic, Hoover-style historical working-group discipline, analogy limits, institutional context, decision points, and present-use constraints so historical comparison can become a bounded case-study memo rather than loose analogy.
fields.built_components: The lab's public surface uses a cream-and-parchment editorial palette with the Belfer / Allison-Ferguson / Hoover source list visible on the surface. The page's structural scaffold is in place; case-study density continues to grow as new cases are worked under the methodology.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: Build case-study depth — each new case worked under the methodology with its source documentation visible — and bind the framework references to specific Belfer and Hoover passages rather than gesturing at them. Long-term, the lab develops alongside the contemporary analytical pages, not separately from them. Step 01 Case-study depth Each new case worked under the methodology with its source documentation visible to the reader. Step 02 Belfer / Hoover binding Framework references bound to specific passages in the Belfer and Hoover corpora rather than gestured at. Step 03 Co-development cadence Develop alongside the contemporary analytical pages rather than separately, so the historical and contemporary tracks stay in conversation.
fields.status: Status: The page's structural scaffold is in place; case-study density continues to grow as new cases are worked under the methodology.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: build case-study depth — each new case worked under the methodology with its source documentation visible — and bind the framework references to specific Belfer and Hoover passages rather than gesturing at them. Long-term, the lab develops alongside the contemporary analytical pages, not separately from them.
fields.detailed_source_vision: A portfolio-native applied-history laboratory. The page treats applied history as a methodology — using historical case study to inform contemporary policy and strategic analysis — rather than as a reading list. Grounded in the Belfer Center Applied History Project, the Allison and Ferguson Applied History Manifesto, the Belfer assignment frameworks, the Hoover History Lab, and the Hoover Applied History Working Group. The page is the place where the methodology is exercised; the underlying claim is that applied history done well is a discipline, not a genre, and the lab is built to demonstrate the discipline rather than to anthologize the field.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: Three lenses applied to historical case material, layered top-down. The methodology lens at the top names how the analytical move is made; the case-studies lens in the middle carries the actual cases worked under that methodology; the frameworks lens beneath them holds the scaffolding and the underlying source corpus from Belfer and Hoover.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: An applied-history lab for using historical cases as disciplined analytic instruments.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It connects portfolio-native case selection, Belfer-style applied-history logic, Hoover-style historical working-group discipline, analogy limits, institutional context, decision points, and present-use constraints so historical comparison can become a bounded case-study memo rather than loose analogy.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It matters because history is useful only when the transfer conditions, institutional differences, decision points, and analogy limits are explicit.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.
